element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for combat before firearms acquired
Core combat tool; ammunition type selection (explosive vs tranquilizer) teaches tactical decision-making
Environmental control and area denial tactics
Defensive/utility progression; increasingly advanced versions unlock with character progression
Risk/reward decision-making; regeneration vs fatigue tradeoffs
Puzzle-solving and spatial navigation in abstract 3D wireframe environment; unlocks locked doors and narrative progression
Vulnerable to EMP weapons; teaches weapon-enemy matchmaking strategy
Vulnerable to gas grenades; resistant to EMP; escalates difficulty curve
Environmental hazards and progression blocking; creates tension through anticipatory threat

element_introduction_order.element: Log discs and email story progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Narrative delivery; discovered throughout station; non-linear storytelling supports exploration

typical_relief_triggers: Cyberspace puzzle completion unlocking physical world progression
Narrative story revelations via log discs
typical_tension_triggers: SHODAN verbal taunts and AI obstruction
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear exploration with episodic tension cycles driven by resource management and enemy encounters; Cyberspace sequences provide cognitive break from combat; SHODAN's continuous presence maintains ambient threat even in empty corridors

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side corridors and alternate passages allow non-linear progression through multi-level space station; dead ends often contain resource caches, rewarding exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Vertical level navigation (up/down through station decks) suggests progression path; environmental density increases toward critical areas; SHODAN-controlled sectors architecturally distinguish themselves from operational station areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Light enemy resistance; basic combat introduction; abundant ammo and health; lead pipe and early projectile weapons available; tutorials for lean/crouch/view controls
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of cyborg and mutant enemies; weapon type specialization becomes critical (EMP vs gas grenades); Cyberspace sequences increase in complexity; resource scarcity rises; multi-enemy encounters more frequent
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained mutant encounters; advanced enemy variants; critical energy management for hardware; Cyberspace puzzles integrate with physical world consequences; SHODAN interference escalates
notable_spikes: First mutant encounter (introduces new enemy type requiring gas grenades)
Complex Cyberspace sequences requiring abstract spatial reasoning
Multi-level boss encounters with mixed enemy types

notable_level_patterns.description: Station organized into interconnected decks accessible via elevators and vertical shafts; player navigates up/down through functional areas (engineering, medical, command); height advantage/disadvantage creates tactical positioning opportunities

Abstract wireframe Cyberspace sequences (puzzles, data collection) have concrete effects on physical station (door unlocks, systems shutdown); creates narrative bridge between digital and physical threat from SHODAN
Dead-end corridors and side chambers contain concentrated weapon/ammo/health pickups; risk-reward of exploration (enemy encounters) vs. supply acquisition
Station areas become associated with specific enemy factions (robot-controlled sectors, mutant-infested zones, cyborg patrols); signals to player which weapon loadout matches regional threats
